quality.rationale: 涉及宇宙学参数演化与参数化解法，逻辑链条完整，具备明确物理背景与可替换条件

origin.question: Problem 19.22. Suppose that at the time of hydrogen recombination (which, say, occurred at a redshift of $z = 1500$ ) the deceleration parameter was $q = 0.5002$ . What would $q_0$ be today? Repeat for $q = 0.4998$ at $z = 1500$ . (Assume a matter-dominated universe.)
origin.solution: Solution 19.22. From the solution of Problem 19.18, if \mathbf{q}_0 > 0.5 we must have a \mathbf{k} = +1 cosmology which expands according to$$R (t) / R_{0} = \frac {q_{0}}{2 q_{0} - 1} (1 - \cos \eta). \tag {1}$$Setting \mathbf{R}(t) = \mathbf{R}_0 , so that \eta = \eta_0 , gives a formula which relates \eta_0 to \mathbf{q}_0 at the present epoch. The epoch is arbitrary, however, so it also relates \eta and \mathbf{q} at any epoch. Putting \mathbf{q} = 0.5002 gives 1 - \cos \eta = .0008 at z = 1500 . Substituting this back into Equation (1) and putting \mathbf{R}_0 / \mathbf{R}(t) = 1 + z = 1501 , we can solve for \mathbf{q}_0 , getting$$\mathrm {q}_{0} = [ 2 - (1 + z) (1 - \cos \eta) ]^{- 1} = 1. 2 5.$$If \mathbf{q}_0 < 0.5 , the \mathbf{k} = -1 analogy of Equation (1) is$$R (t) / R_{0} = \frac {q_{0}}{1 - 2 q_{0}} (\cos h \eta - 1) \tag {2}$$so \mathbf{q} = .4998 gives \cos h \eta - 1 = .0008 at z = 1500 and$$\mathbf {q}_{0} = [ 2 + (1 + z) (\cos h \eta - 1) ]^{- 1} = 0. 3 1 2.$$
